preset_description: Dark = identity signal. Visual language expresses who the user is. Validator gets authority and weight (heavy dark). Mover gets minimal light with one dark CTA punch. Explorer gets all-neutral — open, spacious, no urgency.

brief.audience: SaaS trial Users for an Intent-Based, AI-Ready Design System

sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: Global navigation opens every page. For a Validator at the decision stage, the minimal variant focuses attention on conversion while maintaining wayfinding trust. The persistent CTA in the nav serves as an always-available upgrade path for trial users evaluating whether to commit.

sections.name: Hero — Conversion-Oriented Value Proposition
sections.rationale: The opening section must immediately reaffirm the product's value to a Validator at the decision stage — someone who already understands MODE but needs final confirmation. HeroPrimary's Validator variant includes a trust signal (e.g., customer count or satisfaction metric) right above the fold, anchoring credibility before any scrolling occurs. The CTA drives directly toward paid conversion.

sections.name: Social Proof — Authority and Credibility Bar
sections.rationale: Validators at the decision stage require external validation before committing. SocialProofBar's Validator variant leads with a high-impact quote from a recognized customer, followed by logo clusters. Placed directly after the hero, it intercepts the 'who else trusts this?' question that Validators instinctively ask before evaluating details further down the page.

sections.name: Outcome Metrics — Quantified Proof of Value
sections.rationale: Validators distrust unsupported claims. StatBlock at the decision stage delivers hard metrics — adoption rates, time saved, ROI — with full source attribution as required by the Validator variant. This section converts the product promise from narrative into evidence, bridging the gap between trial experience and the confidence needed to pay.

sections.name: Capability Breakdown — Benefit-Oriented Feature Grid
sections.rationale: A Validator at the decision stage needs structured, scannable proof that the product covers their requirements. FeatureGrid's Validator variant uses outcome-oriented benefit statements rather than feature labels, letting the trial user mentally check off capabilities they've tested against a complete picture of what they'd retain by upgrading.

sections.name: Customer Testimonial — Decision-Point Trust Signal
sections.rationale: TestimonialSingle is purpose-built for Validators and eligible at the decision stage. Placed after features and metrics, it provides narrative social proof — a real person, with role and company, describing their conversion-worthy outcome. The with-photo and with-company-logo variants maximize perceived authenticity. Positioned just before pricing, it primes the upgrade decision.

sections.name: Pricing — Transparent Plan Comparison
sections.rationale: PricingCard is the core decision-stage component. For Validators, the variant with full descriptions and detailed feature lists eliminates ambiguity about what each tier includes — critical for users who need to justify the spend. The highlighted variant marks the recommended plan, and with-comparison supports enterprise-tier differentiation. This is where the trial-to-paid conversion is decided.

sections.name: Final Conversion Banner and Footer
sections.rationale: CTABanner's Validator variant closes with a subhead reinforcing risk-reversal trust signals ('No credit card required', 'Cancel anytime') — the final reassurance a Validator at the decision stage needs before clicking. FooterMinimal with the with-cta variant provides a recovery CTA for users who scroll past pricing without converting, ensuring no departing Validator leaves without a last prompt.

page.reasoning: Conversion-focused decision stage requires 'minimal variant for conversion-focused pages — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only.' Validator archetype at decision funnel stage needs clear path to conversion.

Validator archetype 'always includes trust_signal' and 'with-social-proof variant places LogoStrip immediately below CTACluster,' establishing credibility at the opening moment for decision-stage users.

features.title: Intent-Aware Components
features.description: Components that detect user context and adapt their behavior automatically — reducing one-off overrides by up to 74%. (2024 customer survey)

features.title: AI-Ready Token Architecture
features.description: Design tokens structured for AI tooling handoff so your models and your design system stay in sync without manual reconciliation.

features.title: Role-Based Access Controls
features.description: Protect production components from unreviewed edits with granular permissions that map to your existing team structure.

features.title: Usage Analytics Dashboard
features.description: See which components your team actually uses, where adoption stalls, and which variants underperform — with weekly digest reports.

features.title: Unlimited Workspace Integrations
features.description: Connect Figma, Storybook, GitHub, and your CI pipeline in one authenticated workspace — no separate plugin licensing required.

features.title: Priority Support & Onboarding
features.description: Dedicated onboarding session within 48 hours of upgrade, plus a <4-hour response SLA for support tickets from Pro teams.
